questions.q: On what date was the Election Commission of India established?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India was established on 25 January 1950.

questions.q: Who heads the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is headed by the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: The Election Commission of India is best described as which type of body?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is an autonomous constitutional body.

questions.q: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner and Election Commissioners are appointed by the President.

questions.q: Elections to which offices, besides Parliament and state legislatures, are conducted by the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission administers elections to the offices of President and Vice-President as well as Parliament and state legislatures.

questions.q: Who was the first Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen was the first Chief Election Commissioner, who organised the first two general elections.

questions.q: The model code of conduct enforced by the Election Commission applies during which period?
questions.explanation: The model code of conduct comes into force during the election period to ensure free and fair elections.

questions.q: The Election Commission is responsible for preparing and revising which key document?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission superintends the preparation and revision of electoral rolls.

questions.q: Which Chief Election Commissioner is widely associated with vigorously enforcing the model code of conduct in the 1990s?
questions.explanation: T.N. Seshan, who served from 1990 to 1996, is known for rigorously enforcing the model code of conduct.

questions.q: In which city is the headquarters of the Election Commission of India located?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India is headquartered in New Delhi.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution provides for the Election Commission and vests in it the superintendence of elections?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission and vests in it the superintendence, direction and control of elections.

questions.q: Provisions relating to elections, including the Election Commission, fall under which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part XV of the Constitution deals with elections, including Article 324 on the Election Commission.

questions.q: For most of the period between 1950 and 1989, the Election Commission functioned as a body with how many members?
questions.explanation: From 1950 until 1989 the Election Commission was largely a single-member body headed by the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: Since 1993, the Election Commission has generally functioned as a multi-member body with how many members?
questions.explanation: Since 1993, the Election Commission has been a three-member body comprising the Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners.

questions.q: The Chief Election Commissioner holds office for a term of six years or until reaching which age, whichever is earlier?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner serves a term of six years or until the age of 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.q: The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed from office in the same manner as which other office-holder?
options: A judge of the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only in the same manner and on the same grounds as a judge of the Supreme Court.

questions.q: The Election Commission grants recognition to political parties and allots which to them?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission recognises political parties and allots election symbols to them.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen, as first Chief Election Commissioner, organised India's first general election held over which period?
questions.explanation: The first general election, organised under Sukumar Sen, was held from October 1951 to February 1952.

questions.q: Disqualifications of legislators on grounds of holding an office of profit or related matters are referred to the President, who acts on the opinion of which body?
questions.explanation: Under Articles 103 and 192, the President or Governor acts on the opinion of the Election Commission in such disqualification matters.

questions.q: When the Election Commission is a multi-member body, business is transacted on what basis in case of difference of opinion?
questions.explanation: In a multi-member Election Commission, matters are decided by majority among the members.

questions.q: The conduct of actual elections is governed by laws made by Parliament, principally the Representation of the People Acts of which years?
questions.explanation: The Representation of the People Acts of 1950 and 1951 provide the detailed legal framework for elections.

questions.q: Voter education and the issuance of voter identity cards (EPIC) are functions associated with which body?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission oversees voter education and the issuance of Elector Photo Identity Cards (EPIC).

questions.q: The Election Commission first became a multi-member body briefly in which year before reverting, then becoming permanently multi-member in 1993?
questions.explanation: In 1989 two additional Election Commissioners were appointed for the first time, a change later made permanent in 1993.

questions.q: The conditions of service of the Chief Election Commissioner cannot be varied to their disadvantage after appointment, a safeguard similar to that for which office?
options: A judge of the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: Like a Supreme Court judge, the Chief Election Commissioner's service conditions cannot be altered to their disadvantage after appointment.

questions.q: An Election Commissioner (other than the Chief) can be removed only on the recommendation of which authority?
questions.explanation: An Election Commissioner can be removed only on the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner, giving them a measure of protection.

questions.q: The Election Commission's power under Article 324 has been described by the Supreme Court as plenary in matters where the law is what?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court has held that the Election Commission has plenary powers under Article 324 where existing law is silent.

questions.q: T.N. Seshan, noted for strengthening the Commission's authority, served as Chief Election Commissioner during which period?
questions.explanation: T.N. Seshan served as Chief Election Commissioner from 1990 to 1996.

questions.q: Separate State Election Commissions, distinct from the Election Commission of India, conduct elections to which bodies?
questions.explanation: State Election Commissions, created by the 73rd and 74th Amendments, conduct elections to panchayats and municipalities, separate from the Election Commission of India.

questions.q: The salaries and expenses of the Election Commission, like those of certain other constitutional offices, are intended to be insulated from annual parliamentary vote by being charged on which fund?
questions.explanation: Expenditure on key constitutional offices is charged on the Consolidated Fund of India to protect their independence.

questions.q: The Election Commission organised the first general election for an electorate of over how many eligible voters, most voting for the first time?
questions.explanation: Over 170 million voters were eligible in the first general election of 1951-52, most voting for the first time.
